description_long: This object is a tall, upright inflorescence composed of multiple violet-blue flowers densely clustered along a single green stem. Each flower has a hooded, helmet-like shape with pale, almost white centers, and the petals appear to be fused, forming a tubular structure. The inflorescence is elongated and slightly tapering, with the flowers arranged in a vertical spike, giving it a columnar appearance. The surface of the petals is soft and matte, with subtle textural variations and gentle undulations. At the base, a few slender green stems are visible, but no distinct leaves are present. The overall form is elegant and striking, making it a common ornamental flower in urban parks and gardens.
description: A tall, columnar cluster of violet-blue, hooded flowers with pale centers on a single green stem. The flowers are densely packed in a vertical spike, creating a striking ornamental display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dense, vertical arrangement of violet-blue flowers with pale centers and a clear view of the columnar structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated, slightly curved spike and the overlapping, helmet-shaped flowers.
description_view_2: Direct view of the inflorescence's full height, emphasizing the tight clustering and vertical alignment of the flowers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the layered arrangement of the flowers and the subtle curvature of the spike.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, vertical spike with each flower displaying a bilateral, hooded configuration. The petals are fused, forming a tubular, helmet-like structure with overlapping and layering along the inflorescence.
